channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Australia warns airlines to beware of a Chinese navy live-fire exercise in the Tasman Sea
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia warned airlines flying between Australian airports and New Zealand to beware of Chinese warships conducting a live-fire exercise in the Tasman Sea, Foreign Minister Penny Wong said Friday. Wong confirmed an Australian Broadcasting Corp.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US aid freeze puts HIV-positive orphans in Kenya at risk as medical supplies dwindle
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Two-year-old Evans was brought to the Nyumbani Children’s Home in Nairobi, Kenya a year ago, suffering from HIV and tuberculosis. With no family to care for him, Evans was referred to the orphanage by a health center after he stopped responding to medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS KOMU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: European troops, US support are part of an emerging plan for Ukraine. But it faces many hurdles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As the U.S. and Russia push ahead – so far without Ukraine at the table – on talks to end the war, political and military leaders in Europe are fleshing out details of a plan for European forces to help ensure Moscow does not attack again.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians rally around their president after Trump seeks to denigrate him
item.description: Days before the third anniversary of Russia’s full-scale invasion, Ukrainians are as somber and tense as they were right before Moscow launched the war.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia's top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, MICHELLE GUMEDE and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An encroaching desert threatens to swallow homes and history
item.description: CHINGUETTI, Mauritania (AP) — For centuries, poets, scholars and theologians have flocked to Chinguetti, a trans-Saharan trading post home to more than a dozen libraries containing thousands of manuscripts. But it now stands on the brink of oblivion.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bodies released by Hamas include some of the oldest and youngest hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The bodies of some of the oldest and youngest hostages taken to Gaza at the start of the Israel-Hamas war were released Thursday, bringing the total closer to the 33 supposed to be freed under the ceasefire that started last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel grieves as Hamas turns over the remains of the young Bibas brothers
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Ariel and Kfir Bibas — two of the youngest hostages held in Gaza — became a symbol for Israelis of the brutality of Hamas' Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Facing pressure from Trump, Costa Rica and Honduras join Panama as stopovers for foreign deportees
item.description: SAN JOSÉ, Costa Rica (AP) — A group of families and children hailing from Uzbekistan, China, Afghanistan, Russia and more countries climbed down the stairs of an airplane in Costa Rica's capital Thursday, the first flight of deportees from other nations Costa Rica agreed to hold in detention facilit
item.dc:creator: JAVIER CÓRDOBA and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Terror attack rocks Israel; three buses reportedly explode
item.description: Israel officials have ordered all bus and train services halted while all vehicles are inspected for bombs following two bus explosions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Guterres to propose UN assumes funding for mission struggling to fight gangs in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres said he would request that the United Nations assumes funding for the structural and logistical expenses of a multinational force in Haiti that is struggling to fight violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fearing Trump will cross border militarily to pursue cartels, Mexico moves to reform constitution
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said she will propose constitutional reforms aimed at protecting the country’s sovereignty over concerns that the U.S. military could pursue drug cartels inside Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainians shocked, defiant in face of Trump attacks on Zelensky
item.description: Ukrainian officials feel betrayed but defiant after President Trump this week blamed their president for Russia’s invasion, as the U.S. pursues talks with Vladimir Putin to end the war Russia’s president started three years ago. Desperately reliant on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Vance says Europe 'on the cusp of peace' as Trump pushes for talks with Russia
item.description: NATIONAL HARBOR, Md. — Vice President Vance defended President Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine War on Thursday as the president ramps up his criticism of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: China, Iran and Russia condemned by dissidents at UN watchdog's Geneva summit
item.description: Dissidents from across the globe gathered in Geneva to speak about their experiences fighting oppressive regimes. Family and friends of dissidents spoke as well, some in honor of their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy says he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia’s top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Mogomotsi Magome And Michelle Gumede, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel identifies remains of child hostages but says another body from Hamas was not their mother
item.description: The Israeli military says the remains of two child hostages have been identified but another body released by Hamas was not the boys’ mother.
item.dc:creator: Mohammad Jahjouh, Wafaa Shurafa And Melanie Lidman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italy PM Meloni visits pope in hospital, says he's in good spirits as tests show slight improvement
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni visited Pope Francis for 20 minutes in the hospital Wednesday and reported he was “alert and responsive” and full of good humor, despite his diagnosis of pneumonia and a complicated bronchial infection that has sidelined the 88-year-old pontiff for six days
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, ANDREA ROSA and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Advance of Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo threatens regional peace, UN officials warn
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Top United Nations officials in Africa warned an emergency meeting of the U.N. Security Council on Wednesday that the offensive of Rwanda-backed rebels in mineral-rich eastern Congo is threatening peace in the wider region.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis slept peacefully, ate breakfast following diagnosis of pneumonia in both lungs
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, slept well and ate breakfast on Wednesday morning after he was diagnosed with bilateral pneumonia, according to the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police in disputed Kashmir raid bookstores, seizing books related to Islamic group
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Police in Indian-controlled Kashmir raided bookstores and seized hundreds of books linked to a major Islamic organization in the disputed region, where strict controls on the press have escalated in recent years. The raids began on Friday in Srinagar, the region’s main city.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio makes progress with Russia after Trump promised to bring end to war in Ukraine
item.description: Secretary of State Macro Rubio said first step in ending the war in Ukraine is reestablishing diplomacy with Russia by reopening embassies in Moscow and Washington, as EU watches with concern.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Delta plane crash: Passengers live after escaping ‘upside down’ jet
item.description: Air traffic control audio captured the moment officials realized Delta Flight 4819 was burning on the tarmac.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Delta plane crash: What we know about CRJ-900, aircraft that flipped over in Toronto
item.description: The CRJ-900 can seat either 70 or 76 people, but the layout and basic dimensions for the craft are nearly identical no matter the capacity.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
